name: Wele
description: Wele is the supreme being in Luhya mythology, revered as the creator and sustainer of all things. Wele is often described as a distant and benevolent force, responsible for the creation of the world, humanity, and all living creatures. While not directly involved in the daily affairs of humans, Wele is believed to possess immense power and wisdom, capable of influencing events and providing guidance through intermediaries and natural signs. Some accounts portray Wele as having both male and female aspects, embodying the duality of creation and existence. Wele is not typically represented in physical form, emphasizing the deity's transcendent and intangible nature. Prayers and offerings are sometimes directed toward Wele, particularly during times of crisis or thanksgiving, seeking blessings and protection. The reverence for Wele underscores the Luhya belief in a higher power responsible for the order and harmony of the universe. Wele's influence permeates Luhya culture, shaping moral values and providing a framework for understanding the world and humanity's place within it. Stories and legends about the origins of the world and the first people often invoke Wele as the ultimate source of creation. The concept of Wele represents the Luhya people's understanding of the divine and their connection to the cosmos.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator and Sustainer

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Wele created the world from nothing, shaping the land, the sky, and the waters. Then Wele populated it with plants, animals and finally humans.

keyMyths.title: The First People
keyMyths.description: Wele created the first man and woman, gifting them with the ability to cultivate the land and multiply. They were tasked with caring for Wele's creation.

keyMyths.title: Wele's Distance
keyMyths.description: After creation, Wele withdrew from direct involvement, allowing the world to follow its course. This distance emphasizes human responsibility and free will.

sources.author: Osogo, John
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Luhya history and culture, including religious beliefs.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Luhya history and culture, including religious beliefs.

sources.author: Wandibba, Simiyu
